alerts.title: Iran's supreme leader killed in major US-Israel coordinated strike
alerts.summary: AP News reports Iran's supreme leader has been killed in a joint US-Israel military operation. This represents the most significant escalation in the conflict to date and directly triggered Iran's halt of ongoing nuclear negotiations with the US in Switzerland. Multiple incident reports confirm the strike occurred amid broader military operations (incidents feed).

alerts.title: Iran halts US nuclear talks following Israeli Beirut strike
alerts.summary: Iran has suspended nuclear negotiations with the US following Israeli airstrikes on Beirut, with MSN and multiple outlets confirming the decision. Trump administration has warned Israel against further attacks, fearing collapse of imminent deal. Concurrent developments show US offering to unblock $12 billion in frozen Iranian assets in two tranches to incentivize talks resumption (incidents, telegram).

alerts.title: Israel escalates Lebanon operations; kills two in Sidon airstrike
alerts.summary: Israeli military conducted strike on vehicles in Sidon, Lebanon, killing at least two, contradicting Trump's earlier warning against such attacks. Concurrent Beirut suburbs strikes reported by multiple sources (BBC, NBC News, Al Jazeera). Operations continue despite diplomatic pressure and risks further Iran retaliation (incidents).

alerts.title: Iran and Oman announce joint Strait of Hormuz administration with maritime fees
alerts.summary: Middle East Spectator reports (~48K views) that Iran and Oman have issued joint statement establishing shared control over Strait of Hormuz with new maritime service fees per international standards. This represents strategic shift in regional maritime governance amid US-Iran tensions and could disrupt global shipping (telegram).

alerts.title: IAEA inspectors to visit Iran nuclear sites under interim deal framework
alerts.summary: Multiple sources (IAEA chief statements, UN News, The Independent, wtsp.com) confirm that UN nuclear inspectors will visit Iran's nuclear sites as part of emerging peace agreement. This marks potential breakthrough after months of Iran rejection of IAEA access, though specifics on damaged site inspections remain disputed (incidents, global_metrics).

analysis_note: Strategic landscape has shifted dramatically with Iran's supreme leader killed in US-Israel strike, immediately halting nuclear diplomacy and triggering Iranian retaliation threats; concurrent Israeli operations against Lebanon and Iranian proposals for Hormuz co-control indicate multifront escalation with imminent risk of broader regional conflict.
